prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='CWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='NaiveMovingAverage', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.45) and substantial seasonality variance (0.77), indicating models capture patterns very differently. CWT_catboost leads trend tracking while NaiveMovingAverage dominates seasonal correlation. The early/late horizon split shows meaningful specialization differences, supporting DBA combination as the optimal approach to leverage complementary strengths across horizons.

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "CWT_catboost",

  "overall_champion": "CWT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ARIMA", "late": "CWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["CWT_rf", "rf", "CWT_catboost", "THETA", "ETS", "ARIMA", "FT_svr", "DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "DWT_catboost", "catboost", "ONLY_FT_rf", "svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.4476,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.45) and substantial seasonality variance (0.77), indicating models capture patterns very differently. CWT_catboost leads trend tracking while NaiveMovingAverage dominates seasonal correlation. The early/late horizon split shows meaningful specialization differences, supporting DBA combination as the optimal approach to leverage complementary strengths across horizons."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 4}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 4, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "balanced",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=11 indicates high model diversity across horizons, favoring top-k selection and per-horizon best. relative_spread_mean=0.249 (near 0.25 threshold) suggests significant disagreement, making dba_combination ideal for handling phase-shifted predictions. RMSE spread ratio=0.4476 shows high model disagreement, supporting robust combination methods. n_windows=3 is low, so shrinkage≥0.25 stabilizes inverse_rmse_weights. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination explicitly for high seas_corr variance (0.77). Top-3 models include selection (topk_mean), weighted (inverse_rmse), and baseline (dba) for diversity."
}

CWT_catboost leads trend tracking while NaiveMovingAverage dominates seasonal correlation. The early/late horizon split shows meaningful specialization differences, supporting DBA combination as the optimal approach to leverage complementary strengths across horizons.
